easy.companyDescription: A home-entertainment platform that sells low-margin hardware while trying to grow advertising and distribution revenue.
easy.macroContext: Households are spending more time at home as media companies compete for direct access to viewers.
easy.situation: Can a platform turn a growing installed base into higher-margin advertising revenue before larger technology firms control the living room?
easy.longCase: More viewing and a larger installed base could attract advertisers and content partners to the platform.
easy.shortCase: Hardware economics, platform competition, and negotiations with content suppliers could limit the value of that audience.
setupHints: The company’s software is built into some television sets and external devices.

medium.companyDescription: A consumer technology platform combining an access product with recurring services.
medium.macroContext: At-home use is rising while large ecosystems compete to own customer access and commercial relationships.
medium.situation: Can a growing installed base support recurring economics before larger platforms control distribution?
medium.longCase: More use can improve the value of partner relationships and services relative to the initial product sale.
medium.shortCase: Thin product margins and powerful gatekeepers can limit how much recurring value the platform retains.

hard.companyDescription: A consumer platform with an installed base.
hard.macroContext: A shift in household routines is increasing use of connected services while ecosystem owners tighten control.
hard.situation: Can installed relationships become recurring commercial value before access is commoditized?
hard.longCase: Higher use can deepen partner demand and spread platform costs across more activity.
hard.shortCase: A low-margin entry product and dominant gatekeepers can constrain monetization.

reveal.shortText: That was Roku. At-home viewing increased engagement and helped the company’s platform revenue grow faster than device sales.
reveal.funFact: The company separates its hardware-oriented player business from its higher-margin platform business.
whyItMoved: Advertising and distribution revenue became more important.
Investors valued the potential operating leverage of the platform segment.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance historical data — ROKU
sources.notes: Split-adjusted closes for 2020-01-02 and 2021-01-04; chart series and return.

sources.label: Roku 2020 Form 10-K
sources.notes: Player/platform segments, engagement, advertising, content partners, competition, and hardware economics.

review.reviewNotes: Moderate; adversarial rewrite from clean stored Grok evidence. Changed Medium and Hard plus the identifying Living Room Landgrab title, so Easy/Medium/Hard require rejudge. Removed streaming, media, entertainment interface, advertising, content partners, television, and device-category conjunctions. Installed-base access versus recurring monetization now fits Vizio, Fitbit, Peloton, Amazon, Sonos, and Roku. Stored review.gate2 is preserved and stale by hash.

prohibitedConjunctions: television installed base plus hardware margins

guesses.pointingFact: Low-margin streaming hardware plus ads/distribution, with OS embedded in TVs and external players during the streaming wars.
Sells thin-margin TVs while monetizing the living-room installed base through platform advertising and data.
Fire TV hardware and living-room OS compete for household viewing and ad/distribution economics.
Android TV/Chromecast software in TVs and devices targets the same home-entertainment access-point battle.
tvOS devices and living-room software are another tech platform path into the home, though less ad-hardware driven.

direction.cue: Early-2020 stay-at-home viewing boom and a growing installed base favor converting audience into higher-margin ads despite prior price run-up.

guesses.pointingFact: Access hardware plus recurring ads/services platform sitting between streamers and living-room distribution.
Consumer access devices monetized through platform advertising while larger ecosystems control content relationships.
Hardware access products bundled with growing recurring services amid streaming and ecosystem competition.
Devices plus recurring media/commerce services competing to own customer access relationships.
Consumer access hardware with services upside constrained by platform gatekeepers and thin device margins.

direction.cue: At-home use and installed-base leverage favor recurring platform economics despite prior price run-up.

guesses.pointingFact: Streaming-era consumer access platform with a large installed device base, low-margin hardware, and monetization constrained by dominant ecosystem gatekeepers
Smart-TV installed base used as a streaming access layer where hardware margins are thin and platform ads/partners drive economics
Consumer installed base in connected households facing platform-control pressure while trying to turn usage into recurring value
Consumer platform with scale usage during streaming wars seeking deeper commercial conversion of engagement
Home connected-device platform benefiting from routine shifts while needing recurring monetization beyond the entry product

direction.cue: Early-2020 household connected-service surge should raise usage and partner demand before commoditization fully bites over about a year
